title: daucus carota subsp. rupestris
intro: Daucus carota subsp. rupestris, also known as wild carrot or rock carrot, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant, characterized by its taproot and fern-like foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of carotenoids and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Daucus carota subsp. rupestris has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being consumed raw or cooked, making it a versatile and easily accessible natural remedy.
